---
title: "Page not found"
pagetitle: "404"
description: "The page you were looking for does not exist."
toc: false
anchor-sections: false
---

::: {.text-center .mt-5}
<h1 class="hero-title" style="font-size:clamp(3rem,2rem+6vw,5rem)">404</h1>

This page does not exist. The link may be broken, or the page may have moved.
The search box in the navigation bar covers the whole site.

::: {.btn-row style="justify-content:center"}
<a class="btn-cta" href="/index.html"><i class="bi bi-house"></i> Home</a>
<a class="btn-ghost" href="/software.html"><i class="bi bi-code-square"></i> Software</a>
<a class="btn-ghost" href="/publications/index.html"><i class="bi bi-journal-text"></i> Publications</a>
<a class="btn-ghost" href="/contact.html"><i class="bi bi-envelope"></i> Contact</a>
:::
:::

<!-- NOTE: these links are deliberately root-absolute. GitHub Pages serves 404.html
     for a request at any depth, so a relative link would resolve against the
     missing URL rather than the site root. This is correct for a user site at
     https://<user>.github.io. If you deploy to a PROJECT site
     (https://<user>.github.io/<repo>), prefix each href with /<repo>. -->
